case.title: BROTHERHOOD OF R.R. SIGNALMEN v. SOUTHEASTERN PENNSYLVANIA

opinion.h1: BROTHERHOOD OF R.R. SIGNALMEN v. SOUTHEASTERN PENNSYLVANIA, 489 U.S. 1301 (1989)
opinion.h2: BROTHERHOOD OF RAILROAD SIGNALMEN et al. v. SOUTHEASTERN PENNSYLVANIA TRANSPORTATION AUTHORITY. No. A-715.
Supreme Court of the United States
opinion.p: Applicants request me, as Circuit Justice, to enter an order " immediately dissolving" the injunction issued by the District Court for the Eastern District of Pennsylvania. I deny the application. In my view, applicants have not "established that there is a 'reasonable probability' that four Justices will consider the issue sufficiently meritorious to grant certiorari." Rostker v. Goldberg, 448 U.S. 1306, 1308, 2 (1980) (Chambers Opinion).[ Brotherhood of R.R. Signalmen v. S.E. Penns .Transp. Authority 489 U.S. 1301 (1989) ]
